description: Child was a publication covering child labor legislation, news, and developments. It was established in 1936 and was published monthly by the Children's Department of the U.S. Department of Labor. In 1946, the Bureau was transferred to the Federal Security Agency, a predecessor of the Department of Health and Human Services. The U.S. Children’s Bureau was created in 1912 with a mandate “to investigate and report on all matters relating to the welfare of children and child life among all classes of our people.” Its responsibilities included examining issues such as infant mortality, birth rates, orphanages, juvenile courts, desertion, hazardous occupations, childhood accidents and diseases, child employment, and state and territorial laws affecting children. The publication served as the primary reporting mechanism for the Children's Bureau, disseminating information to professionals, social workers, state and local government agencies, and the public regarding child welfare, health, and labor conditions nationwide.
subject: Social Services And Welfare
Children And Youth - About
title: Child 1936-1953
